title: Memory Fragmentation and Placement Algorithms
summary: Covers the segment placement problem, First/Best/Worst Fit algorithms, external fragmentation, and memory defragmentation.
content_points: Placement algorithms: First Fit (first large-enough free zone), Best Fit (smallest sufficient), Worst Fit (largest).
External fragmentation: many small free zones after repeated alloc/free; total space may suffice but no contiguous zone large enough.
Defragmentation: compact segments into one block, leaving a single large free zone. Time-consuming; updates all descriptors.
Trigger: when placement fails, periodically, or when fragmentation exceeds a threshold.
learning_objectives: Compare First, Best, and Worst Fit algorithms
Identify conditions for running the defragmenter
